seo_title: How Data Centers Can Deliver Higher Performance with Less Hardware
meta_description: Discover how MIT researchers developed a system that boosts storage device performance by intelligently balancing workloads, increasing data center efficiency…
body_html:
Direct answer to the question of how data centers can deliver higher performance with less hardware is that MIT researchers have developed a system that intelligently balances workloads to improve storage device efficiency.

Key BenefitsIncreases overall data center efficiency by balancing workloads across multiple storage devicesReduces the need for specialized hardware, making it more sustainable and cost-effectiveNearly doubles performance delivered by traditional approaches in realistic tasks like AI model training and image compressionSystem ArchitectureComponentDescriptionCentral ControllerMakes big-picture decisions about which tasks each storage device performsLocal ControllersRapidly reroute data if a device is strugglingTwo-Tier ArchitectureHandles three major sources of variability simultaneously, delivering significant speed improvementsReal-World ApplicationsAI model training and image compression tasks saw nearly double the performance delivered by traditional approaches with this system.

source.title: Helping data centers deliver higher performance with less hardware
source.summary: Researchers developed a system that intelligently balances workloads to improve the efficiency of flash storage hardware in a data center.
